case_metadata.court: OHIO FIRST DISTRICT COURT OF APPEALS

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiffs each filed a separate medical-malpractice action against the defendants, arising from a spine surgery performed on them by Dr. Durrani.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiffs filed malpractice actions due to spine surgery by Dr. Durrani.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: verdicts were ultimately entered in favor of each plaintiff on their negligence and fraudulent-misrepresentation claims.
evidence_index.summary: Verdicts favored plaintiffs on negligence and fraud claims.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Steven Bloomfield, a neurosurgeon, testified as to his opinion that Dr. Durrani breached the standard of care in each case by misrepresenting, exaggerating, and/or fabricating certain history and imaging impressions.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Bloomfield testified Durrani misrepresented imaging to justify surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Steven Bloomfield

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Keith Wilkey, an orthopedic surgeon, testified as to his comparison of the original radiologist’s impressions of each plaintiff’s imaging versus Dr. Durrani’s impressions and expressed his opinion as to why he agreed with the radiologist’s impressions.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Wilkey agreed with original radiologist, found Durrani's impressions inaccurate.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Keith Wilkey

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Ms. Jones testified that she experienced back pain on and off since 1959 and went to see Dr. Durrani in 2012 after trying various treatments and medications that never provided lasting relief.
evidence_index.summary: Ms. Jones sought Dr. Durrani's help for chronic back pain in 2012.
evidence_index.speaker: Joan Jones

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Mr. Conley testified that he experienced back pain for over 30 years and went to see Dr. Durrani in 2012 for back and neck pain after physical therapy and chiropractic treatment were unsuccessful.
evidence_index.summary: Mr. Conley consulted Dr. Durrani for unresolved back and neck pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Dana Conley

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The judgments of the trial court are reversed in part as to the awards of prejudgment interest but affirmed in all other respects.
evidence_index.summary: Judgments reversed on prejudgment interest, affirmed otherwise.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The record reflects that, while the trial court did hold a hearing on this issue, no “evidence” was taken at the hearing nor was any evidence submitted with the motions for prejudgment interest.
evidence_index.summary: No evidence was submitted for prejudgment interest motions.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

verdict_detected.key_phrase: The judgments of the trial court are reversed in part as to the awards of prejudgment interest but affirmed in all other respects.

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Ms. Jones experienced back pain on and off since 1959 and sought Dr. Durrani's help in 2012 after various treatments and medications failed to provide lasting relief.

requestables.name: Spinal MRI
requestables.description: MRI of the spine to assess for structural abnormalities

reveal.result_summary: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with chronic back pain considering surgical intervention
decision_points.prompt: What should be the basis for recommending spine surgery?

options.description: Recommend surgery based on accurate imaging and clinical correlation
options.clinical_reasoning: Surgery should be based on accurate and justified clinical findings

options.description: Recommend surgery based on exaggerated or fabricated imaging impressions
options.clinical_reasoning: Misrepresentation of imaging to justify surgery is inappropriate

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant recommended surgery based on misrepresented imaging
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant misrepresented imaging to justify surgery

expected_action_court.description: Surgery should have been recommended based on accurate imaging
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated misrepresentation was a breach

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony E003 and E004
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Surgery should be based on accurate imaging

timeline_phases.name: Initial Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with chronic back pain seeking surgical opinion

key_findings: Breach of duty in misrepresenting imaging to justify surgery

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Accurate imaging interpretation would not have led to surgery

factual_timeline.event: Ms. Jones consulted Dr. Durrani for chronic back pain

tests_performed.result: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

allegations.allegation: Misrepresentation of imaging to justify surgery
